title: seseli gummiferum subsp. crithmifolium
intro: Seseli gummiferum subsp. crithmifolium, also known as Russian lovage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to Central Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Russian lovage include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, Russian lovage is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used as a flavoring agent, due to its distinctive and aromatic flavor profile.
